subject: Physics
title: Ferrofluids: Magnetic Liquids
description: A quick win: understand Ferrofluids: Magnetic Liquids.

hook.text: Ferrofluids are liquids that respond to magnets—forming spiky, alien-looking shapes when a magnet approaches.

buildup.text: They're made of nanoscale iron particles suspended in oil. Each particle is smaller than a bacterium.

discovery.text: NASA invented ferrofluids in the 1960s to control liquid fuel in zero gravity using magnets.

twist.text: The spikes form because surface tension and magnetic force compete—creating beautiful geometric patterns.

climax.text: Today, ferrofluids seal hard drives, dampen speakers, and are being tested for targeted cancer drug delivery.

punchline.text: Magnetic liquid from NASA. Now sealing hard drives and fighting cancer.

quiz.question: Why were ferrofluids originally invented?
options: To control liquid fuel in zero gravity using magnets
To create artistic displays in museums
